---
title: "Registration Benchmark"
subtitle: "Comparing curve registration methods for the tf R package"
---

This benchmark study compares 5 curve registration methods across 15 data-generating
processes (DGPs), varying warp severity and noise levels. It accompanies the
[tf](https://github.com/tidyfun/tf) R package for tidy functional data in R.

## Studies

- **[Study A: Main Results](sim-report-main.qmd)** --- Full factorial comparison
  of 5 methods across 15 DGPs, 2 severity levels, and 3 noise levels (45,000 runs).

- **[Study B: Penalization](sim-report-penalization.qmd)** --- Sensitivity of
  registration quality to the smoothing penalty parameter $\lambda$.

- **[Study C: Grid Resolution](sim-report-grid.qmd)** --- Effect of evaluation
  grid density on method performance.

- **[Study D: Oracle Template](sim-report-oracle.qmd)** --- Isolating estimation
  error by providing the true template function.

- **[Study E: Outlier Contamination](sim-report-outlier.qmd)** --- Robustness of
  methods under outlier contamination.

- **[Study F: Pre-Smoothing](sim-report-presmoothing.qmd)** --- Impact of
  pre-smoothing on SRVF registration.

## Methods

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `srvf` | Square-root velocity function (elastic) registration via `fdasrvf` |
| `cc_default` | Continuous registration with FPC1 cross-correlation criterion |
| `cc_crit1` | Continuous registration with L2-distance criterion |
| `affine_ss` | Affine registration (shift + scale) |
| `landmark_auto` | Landmark registration with automatic landmark detection |

## Design

The benchmark follows the ADEMP framework (Morris et al. 2019):

- **15 DGPs** (D01–D15) with diverse template shapes and warp structures
- **2 severity levels** (0.5, 1.0) controlling warp magnitude
- **3 noise levels** (0, 0.1, 0.3) for additive measurement error
- **100 replications** per cell
- **Metrics**: warp MISE, alignment error, SRSF elastic distance, template MISE
